tasks.title: App Marketing OS: create share-artifact specs for Vista, Nash, and Glow
tasks.description:
First action: Open `memory/app-marketing/share-artifact-roadmap.md` and turn each primary artifact into a build-ready spec: Vista Movie Taste Card, Nash Weekly Ranking Receipt Card, Glow Product Verdict Card.

Why it matters: Product-led share artifacts are the highest-leverage missing acquisition loop; without them, posting is mostly external push instead of user/creator pull.

Done looks like: Three spec files exist under `memory/app-marketing/share-artifacts/` with fields, visual layout, export sizes, data inputs, guardrails, and repo/app implementation notes; follow-up coding tasks are created if app changes are needed.

References:
- memory/app-marketing/share-artifact-roadmap.md
- memory/app-marketing/revised-operating-model-2026-05-19.md

Guardrail: Specs only; do not edit production apps until implementation plan is approved or delegated to a coding agent.
tasks.reason: Product-led share artifacts are the highest-leverage missing acquisition loop; without them, posting is mostly external push instead of user/creator pull.

tasks.title: Vista app change: implement Movie Taste Card export/share artifact
tasks.description:
First action: Review the Vista repo/app structure and create a build plan for a shareable Movie Taste Card export: taste profile, top/controversial ratings, 9:16 + square image export, and CTA.

Why it matters: Vista should not rely only on TikTok/ReelFarm. Letterboxd-style social behavior shows movie taste travels when users can share identity artifacts.

Done looks like: A build-ready implementation plan exists with repo path, screens/components to edit, required data, image export approach, acceptance tests, and one follow-up coding-agent task if needed.

References:
- memory/app-marketing/share-artifact-roadmap.md
- memory/app-marketing/competitor-mining-protocol.md

Guardrail: Do not position as a generic Letterboxd killer. Lead with precision/taste identity.
tasks.reason: Vista should not rely only on TikTok/ReelFarm. Letterboxd-style social behavior shows movie taste travels when users can share identity artifacts.

tasks.title: Nash app change: implement Weekly Ranking Receipt Card export
tasks.description:
First action: Review Nash Satoshi app/repo and scope a shareable weekly ranking receipt card: top movers, model disagreement, undervalued/overhyped leaderboard, methodology note, and X/9:16 export.

Why it matters: Nash needs to behave like a public ranking thesis engine with receipts, not another crypto dashboard.

Done looks like: A build-ready implementation plan exists with data inputs, visual design, safe language, export path, and coding-agent task if changes are needed.

References:
- memory/app-marketing/share-artifact-roadmap.md
- memory/app-marketing/app-registry.md

Guardrail: No financial advice, return claims, or price predictions.
tasks.reason: Nash needs to behave like a public ranking thesis engine with receipts, not another crypto dashboard.

tasks.title: Glow app change: implement safe Product Verdict Card export
tasks.description:
First action: Review Glow Index app/repo and scope a shareable product verdict card: score/rank, formula/value/evidence/claim-check summary, safe suitability language, and TikTok/Pinterest-friendly export.

Why it matters: Glow’s strongest growth loop is likely search + creator shopping demos; creators need a clean share card they can use safely.

Done looks like: A build-ready implementation plan exists with data fields, visual design, claim guardrails, export path, and coding-agent task if changes are needed.

References:
- memory/app-marketing/share-artifact-roadmap.md
- memory/app-marketing/app-registry.md

Guardrail: No medical/dermatology/treatment claims, fake testimonials, before/after, or guaranteed outcomes.
tasks.reason: Glow’s strongest growth loop is likely search + creator shopping demos; creators need a clean share card they can use safely.

tasks.title: App Marketing OS: build borrowed-audience target lists for Vista, Nash, and Glow
tasks.description:
First action: Use `memory/app-marketing/borrowed-audience-playbook.md` to research 20 targets per app: creators, newsletters, app reviewers, community mods, roundup writers, and directory owners.

Why it matters: Borrowed audience is likely the highest-leverage low-budget channel. We need qualified targets before drafting outreach or collab asks.

Done looks like: Files exist under `memory/app-marketing/borrowed-audience/` for Vista, Nash, and Glow with 20 targets each, audience notes, fit rationale, risk, and suggested asset/pitch angle.

References:
- memory/app-marketing/borrowed-audience-playbook.md
- memory/app-marketing/winning-pattern-research-protocol.md

Guardrail: Eve drafts only. JT sends any external messages.
tasks.reason: Borrowed audience is likely the highest-leverage low-budget channel. We need qualified targets before drafting outreach or collab asks.

tasks.title: App Marketing OS: add experiment-card tracking to weekly review
tasks.description:
First action: Update `memory/app-marketing/experiment-calendar.md` or the weekly review process so every promoted pattern uses `experiment-card-template.md` and the 24+/35 scoring rule.

Why it matters: The system should not generate content/task spam from weak swipe ideas. Only scored, measurable experiments should hit Mission Control.

Done looks like: Experiment calendar has a section for pattern score, source proof, tracking/source tag, 24h/72h/7d result, and scale/iterate/kill decision.

References:
- memory/app-marketing/experiment-card-template.md
- memory/app-marketing/winning-pattern-research-protocol.md
- memory/app-marketing/measurement-spine.md

Guardrail: A marketing insight cannot become a Mission Control task unless it has an experiment card or is a clear infrastructure fix.
tasks.reason: The system should not generate content/task spam from weak swipe ideas. Only scored, measurable experiments should hit Mission Control.

tasks.title: App Marketing OS: implement measurement spine/source tags for all new experiments
tasks.description:
First action: Add the fields from `memory/app-marketing/measurement-spine.md` to `post-registry.jsonl` workflow or the weekly experiment review process.

Why it matters: Without source tags and result windows, Eve cannot learn which channels actually produce users.

Done looks like: New experiments created after this task include app, channel, source URL/tag, creative type, CTA, 24h/72h/7d metrics, attribution confidence, and decision.

References:
- memory/app-marketing/measurement-spine.md
- memory/app-marketing/post-registry.jsonl
- memory/app-marketing/weekly-scoreboard.md

Guardrail: Do not scale a channel if attribution is unknown for 2 consecutive weeks.
tasks.reason: Without source tags and result windows, Eve cannot learn which channels actually produce users.

tasks.title: Vista competitor teardown: Letterboxd/social movie app acquisition loops
tasks.description:
First action: Run the competitor mining protocol for Letterboxd, Serializd, TV Time, Likewise, Sequel, JustWatch, and IMDb app/social behavior.

Why it matters: Vista’s wedge must be based on proven movie-app behavior: taste identity, lists, friends/followers, and compatibility/share artifacts.

Done looks like: Save `memory/app-marketing/competitor-intel/vista-movie-app-teardown-YYYY-MM-DD.md` with channels, user complaints, share loops, SEO/ASO patterns, gaps, and at least 3 experiment cards.

References:
- memory/app-marketing/competitor-mining-protocol.md
- memory/app-marketing/experiment-card-template.md

Guardrail: Extract structure only; do not copy creatives or wording.
tasks.reason: Vista’s wedge must be based on proven movie-app behavior: taste identity, lists, friends/followers, and compatibility/share artifacts.

tasks.description:
First action: Run the competitor mining protocol for CoinGecko categories, DeFiLlama, Token Terminal, Messari-style research, crypto newsletters, and AI-token dashboard/search behavior.

Why it matters: Nash needs proof-based distribution and borrowed credibility, not generic crypto-dashboard marketing.

Done looks like: Save `memory/app-marketing/competitor-intel/nash-ranking-thesis-teardown-YYYY-MM-DD.md` with acquisition channels, content formats, partner/newsletter opportunities, trust gaps, and at least 3 experiment cards.

References:
- memory/app-marketing/competitor-mining-protocol.md
- memory/app-marketing/experiment-card-template.md

Guardrail: No return claims, price predictions, or investment advice.
tasks.reason: Nash needs proof-based distribution and borrowed credibility, not generic crypto-dashboard marketing.

tasks.title: Glow competitor teardown: skincare scanner/ranking app acquisition loops
tasks.description:
First action: Run the competitor mining protocol for SkinSort, OnSkin, Yuka, INCIdecoder, Think Dirty, EWG Skin Deep, and Sephora/Ulta review/search behavior.

Why it matters: Glow’s acquisition likely comes from SEO, safe product pages, creator shopping demos, and product verdict artifacts. We need competitor proof before scaling content.

Done looks like: Save `memory/app-marketing/competitor-intel/glow-skincare-app-teardown-YYYY-MM-DD.md` with channels, review complaints, SEO patterns, creator/demo formats, gaps, and at least 3 experiment cards.

References:
- memory/app-marketing/competitor-mining-protocol.md
- memory/app-marketing/experiment-card-template.md

Guardrail: Avoid medical/treatment framing and do not copy creator scripts.
tasks.reason: Glow’s acquisition likely comes from SEO, safe product pages, creator shopping demos, and product verdict artifacts. We need competitor proof before scaling content.

tasks.title: Vista: submit first durable directory listing from existing pack
tasks.description:
First action: Open `memory/app-marketing/directory-packs-vista.md`, pick 3-5 screenshots from `~/projects/jtsomwaru-com/public/images/vista/`, then submit Vista to Uneed first.

Why it matters: Vista is live and already has a precision-rating SEO page; a durable listing/backlink compounds while TikTok/ReelFarm metrics are still thin.

Done looks like: One Vista listing is submitted, the submitted URL is pasted back, and `memory/app-marketing/directory-submissions.md` is updated with status + URL.

References:
- memory/app-marketing/directory-packs-vista.md
- memory/app-marketing/directory-submissions.md
- memory/app-marketing/seo-briefs-vista-1-100-movie-rating-app.md

Guardrail: No paid submissions without approval. Do not overclaim; position Vista as precise/private taste tracking, not a Letterboxd replacement for everyone.
tasks.reason: Vista is live and already has a precision-rating SEO page; a durable listing/backlink compounds while TikTok/ReelFarm metrics are still thin.

tasks.title: Vista: add App Store vendor number for reporting metrics
tasks.description:
First action: Securely add `APPSTORE_VENDOR_NUMBER` to the approved env/config surface, then run `python3 scripts/app_marketing_connectors/app_store_metrics.py` and `python3 scripts/app_marketing_collect_metrics.py`.

Why it matters: Vista App Store metadata auth works and connector readiness exists, but Apple sales/reporting metrics require vendor number/reporting permission before downloads or product-page metrics can flow into the scoreboard.

Done looks like: `memory/app-marketing/app-store-metrics-status.json` reports `reporting_status: sales_report_ready` or a precise Apple-side permission/agreement blocker; Vista App Store rows appear in `memory/app-marketing/metrics-inbox.jsonl` when reporting is available.

References:
- memory/app-marketing/app-store-metrics-status.json
- memory/app-marketing/analytics-access-needed.md
- scripts/app_marketing_connectors/app_store_metrics.py

Guardrail: Never paste Apple private key contents, vendor number, or account secrets into docs/chat; if Apple-side agreement/role changes are required, stop and create a one-time JT action.
tasks.reason: Vista App Store metadata auth works and connector readiness exists, but Apple sales/reporting metrics require vendor number/reporting permission before downloads or product-page metrics can flow into the scoreboard.

tasks.title: Vista: run ASO baseline and one metadata/screenshot test recommendation

First action: Capture current Vista App Store title, subtitle, first 3 screenshots, description first 3 lines, and compare against Letterboxd/IMDb/Serializd/indie movie trackers.

Why it matters: Vista is an App Store product; ASO is a durable acquisition lever that does not depend on ReelFarm cold starts.

Done looks like: A short ASO report is saved under `memory/app-marketing/vista-aso-baseline-YYYY-MM-DD.md` with one metadata test and one screenshot test JT can apply.

References:
- memory/app-marketing/aso-checklist.md
- memory/app-marketing/app-registry.md

Guardrail: Do not change App Store Connect directly unless JT explicitly approves.
tasks.reason: Vista is an App Store product; ASO is a durable acquisition lever that does not depend on ReelFarm cold starts.

Why it matters: Prior data says rating precision beat vague hooks; the OS needs one clean test with captured metrics before increasing volume.

Done looks like: One Vista ReelFarm post is queued/posted, then views/saves/comments are logged into `memory/app-marketing/post-registry.jsonl` or metrics inbox within 72 hours.

tasks.title: Glow Index: fix/verify crawler access for sitemap, llms.txt, rankings, and categories
tasks.description:
First action: Run `python3 scripts/glow_crawler_check.py` to capture the current blocker; then use `memory/app-marketing/glow-crawler-access-2026-05-13.md` to add narrow Cloudflare/Replit skip rules for `/robots.txt`, `/sitemap.xml`, `/llms.txt`, `/rankings`, `/categories`, and `/categories/*`.

Why it matters: Read-only checks found 403 Cloudflare challenges on Glow discovery/category routes; AI/search visibility fails if crawlers cannot reach sitemap, llms.txt, or category pages.

Done looks like: `python3 scripts/glow_crawler_check.py` exits 0 and `memory/app-marketing/glow-crawler-access-status.json` shows all six URLs `ok: true`; update the report with verified results.

References:
- memory/app-marketing/glow-crawler-access-2026-05-13.md
- memory/app-marketing/durable-discovery-plan.md
- memory/app-marketing/pseo-geo-module-spec-glow-index.md

Guardrail: Do not expand programmatic pages until crawler access and safe-claim templates are healthy.
tasks.reason: Read-only checks found 403 Cloudflare challenges on Glow discovery/category routes; AI/search visibility fails if crawlers cannot reach sitemap, llms.txt, or category pages.

tasks.description:

tasks.reason: Glow should not receive social volume until there is a way to measure visits/searches/clicks and learn from it.

tasks.title: App Marketing OS: implement weekly self-improvement decision rules
tasks.description:
First action: Add a weekly review step that reads metrics + experiment calendar + generated MC task outcomes and outputs continue/kill/rework decisions per app/channel.

Why it matters: Autonomy without kill/continue rules becomes stale task spam. The OS needs to stop dead channels and double down on evidence.

Done looks like: A self-improvement rule file exists and the weekly App Marketing review prompt/script references it before generating new tasks.

References:
- memory/app-marketing/os-spec.md
- memory/app-marketing/weekly-scoreboard.md
- memory/app-marketing/experiment-calendar.md

Guardrail: Never auto-increase volume when metrics are missing for 2 consecutive weeks; fix measurement first.
tasks.reason: Autonomy without kill/continue rules becomes stale task spam. The OS needs to stop dead channels and double down on evidence.
